name: DeepSeek-R1-Zero
archDisplay: DeepSeekMoE: 256 routed + 1 shared expert, top-8 routing, 61 layers (3 dense + 58 MoE), Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA)
note: Trained by pure large-scale RL on V3-Base with no SFT cold start; first open proof reasoning can emerge from RL alone, but suffers repetition/readability/language-mixing issues.
tpsNote: Same 671B MoE footprint as DeepSeek-R1; does not fit a single 24GB 4090 even at Q4, and community quantization interest has been lower than for the instruction-tuned R1.

results.notes: ARC Prize Foundation's independent semi-private-set evaluation, published by Mike Knoop on 2025-01-29 as 'An Analysis of DeepSeek's R1-Zero and R1'. Table reports R1-Zero (No SFT / no search) at 14% on ARC-AGI-1 (avg ~11K tokens, ~$0.11/task), versus R1's 15% on the same set — not in DeepSeek's own paper/HF card, which does not report ARC-AGI at all.

verificationNotes: Existence confirmed via live HF model card (deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Zero) and the arXiv paper (2501.12948), which explicitly describes it as trained via pure RL with no SFT. MIT license and 671B/37B params confirmed on HF. All 5 result rows confirmed exactly matching Table 2 of the paper (fetched arxiv.org/html/2501.12948v1).
